codeReview.summary: Implementation provides pattern learning from CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md files with higher trust weight (0.95 base confidence) than agent self-learned patterns (0.4-0.7). The implementation correctly integrates with the existing convention system via ConventionSource::FromOperatorFeedback and uses mtime-based change detection for sync.

issues.description: extract_keywords() may add duplicate keywords if a code term also appears multiple times in the content. The code adds terms from code_terms array AND words appearing 2+ times - same word could be added twice. Consider using a HashSet or checking for duplicates before adding.

extract_examples() only looks for backtick pairs on the same line, missing multi-line code blocks (```code```). Most markdown parsers support fenced code blocks - consider handling triple-backtick blocks in the future for completeness.

sharedStateObservations.observation: ConventionSource::FromOperatorFeedback was already defined in pattern_learning.rs before this feature. The worker correctly reused it rather than creating a duplicate enum variant.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: pattern_learning.rs defines 'FromOperatorFeedback' and operator_feedback.rs imports and uses it correctly

summary: Feature implementation passes review. The operator feedback module correctly parses CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files with 0.95 base confidence (higher than learned patterns at 0.4-0.7), integrates with the existing convention system, and provides mtime-based change detection. Two minor non-blocking observations: potential duplicate keywords in extract_keywords(), and limited code block extraction. All 19 tests pass.
